live_fields.value: FY26/CY2026 capex guidance ~$190B (CFO Hood, Q3 FY26 call 2026-04-29): >$40B in the current quarter (record), ~$190B for calendar 2026; includes leased capacity + ~$25B component inflation. Cash PP&E run-rate ~$107B annualized (9mFY26 PP&E $80.1B). Confirmed still current as of 2026-06.

OpenAI relationship RESTRUCTURED 2026-04-27 (announced ahead of Q3 FY26): MSFT IP license shifts exclusive -> non-exclusive (extended to 2032); MSFT stops paying revenue-share to OpenAI while OpenAI's revenue-share TO MSFT continues to 2030 with a cap; MSFT remains OpenAI's primary cloud partner (OpenAI ships first on Azure); MSFT retains ~27% diluted stake; AGM-clause obligation removed. Net mildly POSITIVE for MSFT AI product economics / reduced capacity exposure. NOTE: dossier O3 (facts.md) still frames OpenAI economics as an un-extracted 'black box' as of as_of -- the dated 2026-04-27 restructuring should be folded in; it does NOT change the price or the STARTER verdict (O3 was the reason MSFT is capped at STARTER not lifted to CORE; the restructuring narrows but does not fully close the primary-economics gap).
